fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Maryland Physical Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a physical therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Maryland - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: physical therapist license reciprocity maryland, how to transfer physical therapist license to maryland, physical therapist compact maryland, maryland physical therapist endorsement application

board.name: Maryland Board of Physical Therapy Examiners
board.mailing_address:
Maryland Board of Physical Therapy Examiners
4201 Patterson Avenue, 4th Floor
Baltimore, MD 21215-2299

license_types.title: PT, DPT
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a physical therapist in Maryland.

steps.title: Create an elicensing account
steps.description: Navigate to the Maryland elicensing portal (mdelect.md.gov/licensing/default.aspx) and click 'Register for an Account' to create your user ID and password.

steps.title: Submit the online application
steps.description: Log into your elicensing account, select 'Apply for a New License,' and complete the Physical Therapist application form, paying the associated application fee.

steps.title: Submit official transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts confirming your physical therapy education to be sent directly from your educational institution to the Maryland Board of Physical Therapy Examiners.

steps.title: Complete a criminal history records check
steps.description: Undergo a criminal history records check by submitting fingerprints to an approved vendor, as instructed by the Board, and ensure results are sent to the Board.

steps.title: Pass the National Physical Therapy Examination (NPTE)
steps.description: Register for and pass the NPTE, administered by the Federation of State Boards of Physical Therapy (FSBPT). The Board will receive your scores directly from FSBPT.

steps.title: Receive license
steps.description: The Maryland Board of Physical Therapy Examiners will review all submitted documents. Once all requirements are met and verified, your license will be issued, and you will receive notification.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination).

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NPTE. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Maryland physical therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination) is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Maryland have reciprocity for physical therapists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Maryland participates in the PT Compact. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Maryland without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my physical therapist license to Maryland?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Maryland while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Physical Therapist in Maryland
hero.dek: Applicants for Physical Therapist licensure in Maryland must apply online through the state's elicensing system.

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the elicensing portal, click 'Register for an Account,' and follow the prompts to create a user ID and password. Once registered, log in and select 'Apply for a New License.'
